articles.title: WHATSAPP PLANS A BIG UI REDESIGN, HERE'S WHAT IT LOOKS LIKE
articles.summary: WhatsApp is about to go through a considerable revamp that includes updated Material Design 3 UI elements and a more modern look and feel. There are also structural changes. New filters will allow you to see your unread, personal, and business messages.

articles.title: STARK ADDS ARIA NOTES AND TOUCH TARGETS TO ENHANCE HANDOFF
articles.summary: Designing with accessibility in mind is highly facilitated by using Stark's plugin for Figma and Sketch. The process has become even more integrated into the way developers enhance accessibility. Stark’s plugin now supports ARIA notes and touch target indications.

articles.title: AN EARLY GOOGLE CHROME WEB STORE PREVIEW SHOWS A 'MODERN' REDESIGN
articles.summary: The Google Chrome Web Store redesign brings its aesthetic closer to the Play Store. The timeframe of its official arrival isn’t clear yet, but Google is asking users and developers for feedback.

articles.title: USER DRIVEN UI
articles.summary: When a product matures and covers more scope, complexity tends to follow. However, does it need to be this way? The advent of AI might provide the answer for evolving products without hurting the experience for new users.

articles.title: HOW TO PICK MATCHING COLOR PALETTES EVERY TIME
articles.summary: You don't need plugins or color generators to create balanced color palettes. This practical guide helps you find equivalent colors in multiple hues based on the HSB color model.

articles.title: PLATFORMS
articles.summary: How does Spotify build products for mobile devices, TVs, and websites that look and feel the same? The answer is Encore, its design system combined with cross-team collaboration with representatives for each platform.

articles.title: A MANIFESTO FOR MOVING BEYOND CREATIVITY
articles.summary: Transitioning from a designer to a design leader requires a shift in mindset and the development of new skills and competencies. Now that designers have a seat at the table, they need to understand their role as design leaders, improve their communication with other stakeholders, contribute to building a solid team culture, and lead by example.

articles.title: SPRIG INSTANTLY TURNS USER FEEDBACK INTO INSIGHTS WITH AI ANALYSIS
articles.summary: AI Analysis for Surveys is a new feature for Sprig that uses generative AI large language models to go through survey data intelligently. This functionality allows the platform to provide instantaneous insights.

articles.title: WHAT’S NEW IN LIGHTHOUSE 11
articles.summary: Thirteen new accessibility audits have been added to Lighthouse since version 10. Manual audits are now available after the automatic audits are completed to help developers make their sites more accessible.

articles.title: PERSONAS NON GRATA
articles.summary: Every product will have not-so-ideal users, so anti-personas should be something to consider! This quick and funny guide may help you to avoid tricky situations down the road.

articles.title: WHAT IS A TOOLTIP?
articles.summary: This deep dive shows how tooltips can involve multiple aspects and design decisions. Packed with examples, the article outlines best practices and what you should consider when designing and adopting them.

articles.title: ELEVEN EXPERIENCE DESIGN LESSONS FROM ADOBE DESIGN SUMMIT
articles.summary: The biennial, multi-day celebration of Adobe's internal design culture is a powerful way to spread its design ethos across the company. But now it's beyond that. Check out the lessons from those who attended the event.

articles.title: CHANGES OVER THE LAST 25 YEARS OF UX RESEARCH
articles.summary: The UX research field has changed considerably since 1998. UX education expanded, usability became user experience, and job titles changed. Many businesses began to see the value of design and research. UX research now occurs earlier in the design process, and the number of UX jobs has increased significantly.

articles.title: FOUR DESIGN PRINCIPLES WE CAN LEARN FROM RAY-BAN
articles.summary: Ray-Ban stayed relevant for eight decades in a very crowded space. Part of that success is related to the solid design culture that guides the company.

articles.title: TIME AS SONG TITLES
articles.summary: This fascinating online clock plays a song with the exact time in its title. It brings music from Spotify based on your local time.

articles.title: POINTING FINGERS
articles.summary: A neat trick to read way faster. Just don't tell your teacher.
